steps.title: Find the sentence boundary
steps.content: The text first defines "hwæt" as an Old English exclamation. The next idea begins with a participial phrase, "Translated as 'hark!' or 'listen!' in some versions," and then has the independent clause "the word was playfully rendered as 'bro!'"

steps.title: Separate the two ideas
steps.content: A leaves a comma splice by joining the completed definition to the next independent clause. B creates an awkward and incomplete parallel structure. C fuses the definition and translation phrase without needed punctuation. A period after "exclamation" correctly separates the definition from the next sentence. The answer is D.
